summary: The most operationally significant development is the US Senate's 50-48 vote on Tuesday in favor of a joint resolution to halt US military action against Iran, a bill that previously passed the House of Representatives. Trump claims a deal has been reached to end Strait of Hormuz hostilities, stating 19 million barrels of oil transited the strait yesterday, which he called the highest volume in its history. Meanwhile, fighting continues on multiple fronts: an IDF soldier was injured by a projectile in Lebanon, and two Hezbollah operatives were struck in the Ras Ali Tahr area.
key_points: The US Senate voted 50-48 on Tuesday for a joint resolution to stop US military action against Iran, following earlier passage in the House of Representatives. (Source: @VahidOnline/76635)

Trump stated that 19 million barrels of oil transited the Strait of Hormuz yesterday, calling it the highest volume in the strait's history and claiming a deal has been reached to end the conflict there. (Source: @VahidOnline/76635)

Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi stated that access to attacked nuclear facilities and enriched uranium will be examined and discussed only within the framework of a final agreement with the US. (Source: @asafroz/50686)
Trump said Iran has informed the US that no tolls, insurance costs, or any other charges are being demanded or received for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4246)

CBS News reported that dozens of ships have transited the Strait of Hormuz following temporary corridor openings announced by Oman and the International Maritime Organization. (Source: @SharghDaily/167917)
An Israeli security official stated that US inspectors will enter Iran alongside IAEA inspectors to locate enriched uranium. (Source: @pkpoi/142955)
An IDF soldier was evacuated to hospital from a projectile incident in Lebanon; his condition is stable. (Source: @pkpoi/142959)

IDF Spokesperson stated that Givati Brigade forces operating in the Ras Ali Tahr area identified and struck two armed Hezbollah operatives who posed a threat to forces in the security zone. (Source: @pkpoi/142951)

sections.executive_summary: The US Senate voted 50-48 on Tuesday for a joint resolution to halt US military action against Iran, following earlier passage in the House of Representatives. Trump claims a deal has been reached to end Strait of Hormuz hostilities, stating 19 million barrels of oil transited the strait yesterday, the highest volume in its history. Meanwhile, fighting continues on multiple fronts: an IDF soldier was injured by a projectile in Lebanon, and two Hezbollah operatives were struck in the Ras Ali Tahr area.

points: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi stated that access to attacked nuclear facilities and enriched uranium will be examined and discussed only within the framework of a final agreement with the US. (Source: @asafroz/50686)
Iran informed the US that no tolls, insurance costs, or any other charges are being demanded or received for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4246)
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf met with Turkish Grand National Assembly Speaker Numan Kurtulmuş on the sidelines of the PUIC conference in Baku. (Source: @PressTV/195460)
Iran tabled a draft proposal to establish a Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) radio and TV union. (Source: @PressTV/195456)

Tehran MP Kamran Ghanbari criticized the continued closure of the public parliamentary chamber, claiming Speaker Ghalibaf has prevented open sessions for four months without legal basis. (Source: @VahidOnline/76649)
Trump stated that Iran has been left without a navy, air force, anti-aircraft defense, missile capability, or nuclear program under the deal. (Source: @VahidOnline/76635)
US Secretary of State Rubio stated that Israel is not in violation of the agreement with Iran and denied reports that Israel seeks to weaken the current understanding. (Source: @SharghDaily/167918)

IDF Spokesperson stated that Givati Brigade forces operating in the Ras Ali Tahr area identified and struck two armed Hezbollah operatives who posed a threat to forces in the security zone. (Source: @pkpoi/142951)
An IDF soldier was evacuated to hospital from a projectile incident in Lebanon; his condition is stable. (Source: @pkpoi/142959)

IDF Spokesperson stated that earlier today IDF forces raided the Kerem Shalom crossing area on suspicion of a Hamas operative on the Gaza side; one unarmed suspect was arrested and transferred for interrogation. (Source: @idf_telegram/24430)

IDF Spokesperson stated that earlier this week IDF forces struck and killed Akram Muhammad Mahmoud Abu Machi, a Hamas military wing operative who infiltrated Israeli territory during the October 7 massacre, in southern Gaza Strip. (Source: @idf_telegram/24432)

Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated at the MUNI EXPO 2026 conference that Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon regardless of American demands, noting 200,000 residents will not return. (Source: @almogboker78/17580)
Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated that the Houthi leader is hiding in tunnels and if he comes into the crosshairs, he will die. (Source: @amitsegal/62498)

Duvdevan unit fighters killed Muhammad Zaid in the village of Yamon in Menashe; according to the security establishment he was involved in terrorist activity and arms trading. (Source: @N12chat/214505)

IDF Spokesperson stated that earlier this week IDF forces struck and killed Akram Muhammad Mahmoud Abu Machi, a Hamas military wing operative who infiltrated Israeli territory during the October 7 massacre, in southern Gaza Strip. (Source: @idf_telegram/24432)

IDF Spokesperson stated that earlier today IDF forces raided the Kerem Shalom crossing area on suspicion of a Hamas operative on the Gaza side; one unarmed suspect was arrested and transferred for interrogation. (Source: @idf_telegram/24430)

The father of Lilah Jahjah, a 9-year-old girl killed by gunfire last month, is expected to be charged with her murder; police have evidence he fired at least 20 bullets toward his two children during a family dispute. (Source: @N12chat/214499)

IDF Spokesperson stated that Givati Brigade forces operating in the Ras Ali Tahr area identified and struck two armed Hezbollah operatives who posed a threat to forces in the security zone. (Source: @pkpoi/142951)
An IDF soldier was evacuated to hospital from a projectile incident in Lebanon; his condition is stable. (Source: @pkpoi/142959)
Lebanese media reported two dead in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon; Lebanon's National News Agency reported the strike targeted a car near Kafr Rumman. (Source: @manniefabian/58674)
Hezbollah published documentation of fighters being hit in southern Lebanon using a drone. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93197)

Israel and Lebanon are discussing a US-backed pilot project to transfer control of select southern Lebanese territories from the IDF to the Lebanese Armed Forces, with participating Lebanese troops undergoing US training and security vetting. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4246)

Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated at the MUNI EXPO 2026 conference that Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon regardless of American demands, noting 200,000 residents will not return. (Source: @almogboker78/17580)
Hussein Al-Oliwi, a senior ISIS commander, was killed by the US military in an airstrike in northern Syria on Friday. (Source: @yediotnews25/131878)

CBS News reported that dozens of ships have transited the Strait of Hormuz following temporary corridor openings announced by Oman and the International Maritime Organization. (Source: @SharghDaily/167917)

sections.threat_assessment: The US Senate's 50-48 vote to halt military action against Iran represents a significant political constraint on the administration's Iran policy, though the operational implications remain uncertain. On the ground, active hostilities continue across multiple fronts: Hezbollah operatives were struck in southern Lebanon's Ras Ali Tahr area, an IDF soldier was injured by a projectile in Lebanon, and a Hamas operative was killed in southern Gaza. The proposed US-backed pilot project to transfer southern Lebanese territories from IDF to Lebanese Armed Forces control could reshape the security architecture along the Israel-Lebanon border if implemented. Iran's military capacity has been severely degraded according to Trump's claims, but Iranian officials continue to negotiate terms regarding nuclear facility access and enriched uranium within the framework of a final agreement.
sections.regional_response: The US legislative pushback against military action in Iran signals growing domestic opposition to the administration's Middle East policy, with the Senate resolution passing 50-48 on Tuesday. Oman and the International Maritime Organization announced a temporary corridor opening for ships to exit the Strait of Hormuz, with CBS reporting dozens of vessels have already transited. Israel's Defense Minister Katz publicly rejected any withdrawal from southern Lebanon, signaling Israeli resistance to US pressure on this front. Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi maintained a firm negotiating posture, conditioning nuclear facility access on a comprehensive final agreement. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf engaged in diplomatic meetings with Turkish officials on the sidelines of the PUIC conference in Baku.
sections.intelligence_notes: The claim of 19 million barrels transiting the Strait of Hormuz in a single day, if accurate, would represent an unprecedented volume that warrants independent verification. The US Senate resolution on halting military action against Iran passed 50-48, indicating narrow partisan division on the issue. Reports of a US-backed pilot project for Lebanese Armed Forces to assume control of select southern Lebanese territories from the IDF have not been officially confirmed by Israeli or Lebanese authorities. The continued closure of Iran's public parliamentary chamber, now reportedly lasting four months according to Tehran MP Ghanbari, suggests internal political tensions related to the negotiation process and ceasefire discussions.
